name: twscrape
description: Python library for scraping Twitter/X data using GraphQL API with account rotation and session management. Use when extracting tweets, user profiles, followers, trends, or building social media monitoring tools.
capabilities: Search tweets by query with GraphQL API
Extract user profiles, followers, and following lists
Collect tweets, replies, and media from any user
Handle multiple accounts with automatic rotation
Export data to JSON or SQLite formats
author: 2025Emma
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown files (SKILL.md, references/*.md, skill-report.json). All 176 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the scanner flagged code snippets within markdown documentation as executable code. The skill itself contains no executable code, no network calls, no file system access, and no code execution paths. It only provides documentation and guidance on using the external twscrape Python library for legitimate Twitter/X data extraction.
valueStatement: Need to extract tweets, user profiles, or trends from Twitter/X? This skill provides complete guidance on using twscrape with account rotation, async operations, and rate limit handling for reliable data collection.
userTitle: Scrape Twitter/X data with GraphQL API
limitations: Requires Twitter/X accounts for API access
Rate limits apply per account (resets every 15 minutes)
Maximum 3,200 tweets per user account (Twitter platform limit)
Account suspension risk with aggressive scraping

useCases.title: Build social media datasets
useCases.description: Collect tweet datasets for academic research, sentiment analysis, or trend detection projects.

useCases.title: Monitor brand mentions
useCases.description: Track conversations about topics, brands, or competitors across Twitter/X in real time.

useCases.title: Integrate Twitter data
useCases.description: Add Twitter/X data collection to applications, dashboards, or automation workflows.

promptTemplates.title: Basic tweet search
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to search for tweets matching "artificial intelligence" and collect the latest 50 results using twscrape.

promptTemplates.title: User analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I get user profile information, follower count, and recent tweets for a Twitter account like "elonmusk"?

promptTemplates.title: Account setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain how to add multiple Twitter accounts to twscrape with cookies for stable authentication and automatic rotation.

promptTemplates.title: Batch collection
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a complete workflow using twscrape to collect tweets from 100 users, handle rate limits, and export everything to a JSON file with error handling.

bestPractices: Use cookie-based authentication for more stable account access
Add multiple accounts to enable rotation and avoid rate limits
Implement proper error handling and retry logic for production use
antiPatterns: Using single account for high-volume scraping without rotation
Ignoring rate limits and continuing requests after hitting limits
Storing account credentials in plaintext without encryption

faq.question: Is twscrape compatible with Twitter/X API v2?
faq.answer: Twscrape uses GraphQL endpoints, not the official API. It scrapes data directly from Twitter/X web interface.

faq.question: What are the rate limits?
faq.answer: Rate limits reset every 15 minutes per endpoint per account. Limits vary by account age and verification status.

faq.question: How do I integrate with Claude Code?
faq.answer: Import twscrape in your Claude Code workflow to add Twitter data collection to AI-powered research and analysis tasks.

faq.question: Is data collection legal?
faq.answer: Collecting publicly available data may be legal in your jurisdiction. Review Twitter/X terms of service before use.

faq.question: Why am I getting no results?
faq.answer: Check account status for suspension, verify query syntax uses Twitter search operators, and try different accounts.

faq.question: How does twscrape compare to official API?
faq.answer: Twscrape accesses more data without API costs but carries suspension risk. Official API is stable but has usage limits.
